title: UHC Service Coverage and RMNCH Mortality Outcomes
title_scene_1: Maternal Mortality​ Rate vs UHC Index
title_scene_2: Under-5 Mortality Rate vs UHC Index
title_scene_3: Neonatal Mortality Rate vs UHC Index

scenes.text: [emphasis: Sierra Leone] and [emphasis: Ethiopia ]achieved rapid declines in [emphasis: maternal mortality] alongside steady improvements in [emphasis: service coverage], highlighting the role of expanded maternal care — including increased use of antenatal and delivery services with skilled health personnel and strengthened primary health care outreach through community health workers.

[emphasis: Rwanda ]and [emphasis: Malawi ]combined strong investments in primary health care and child health programs with [emphasis: rising UHC] scores, contributing to sharp reductions in [emphasis: under-five mortality].
[emphasis: Cambodia], [emphasis: India], and [emphasis: China ]strengthened maternal and newborn services while expanding coverage, supporting sustained improvements in [emphasis: neonatal survival].
